mechanism_narrative: Persistent high inflation, as measured by CPIAUCSL, erodes corporate profit margins and increases the discount rates applied to future equity cash flows, leading to sustained negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 60% of periods where CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 5%, or Rank IC < -0.03 over any 2-year rolling window in such periods.

mechanism_narrative: A significant and sustained increase in the unemployment rate (UNRATE) signals a deteriorating labor market and impending economic recession, which negatively impacts corporate earnings and drives S&P 500 forward returns lower over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE rises by 0.5 percentage points or more over a 3-month period
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 60d return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous and sustained decline in retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) indicates a broad contraction in consumer demand and investment, signaling a weakening economy that will lead to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS and HOUST both decline by more than 2% month-over-month for two consecutive months
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 20d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC for the combined signal is below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) above a critical threshold indicates an aggressive monetary tightening cycle, which raises borrowing costs for corporations and consumers, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: FEDFUNDS rises above 2.0% and shows a 3-month annualized increase of at least 100 basis points
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in > 50% of triggered instances during tightening cycles, or the average return is not significantly negative.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent and robust growth in non-farm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflects a strong labor market, which underpins consumer confidence and spending, thereby supporting corporate revenues and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over a short 5-day horizon.
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 5d returns in > 50% of periods where PAYEMS shows positive month-over-month growth, or Rank IC < 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A clear acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) following a period of stagnation or decline signals a rebound in manufacturing and overall economic activity, which translates into improved corporate outlooks and positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO month-over-month growth turns positive after two consecutive negative months
failure_condition: SPX produces negative 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20d return in triggered periods is not significantly positive (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: A combination of decelerating GDP growth (GDPC1) and rising interest rates (FEDFUNDS) creates a challenging environment for equities, as slower economic expansion limits earnings growth while higher rates increase discount factors, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over a 60-day horizon.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth falls below 2% while FEDFUNDS rises above 1.5%
failure_condition: The joint signal fails to predict negative 60d returns in > 55% of triggered instances, or the Rank IC for the combined signal is below -0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A persistently low unemployment rate (UNRATE) coupled with high and rising consumer price inflation (CPIAUCSL) indicates an overheating economy, forcing the Federal Reserve to implement aggressive monetary policy, which negatively impacts S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: UNRATE remains below 4.0% AND CPIAUCSL annual growth exceeds 6.0%
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20d returns in > 50% of triggered instances, or the average 20d return in triggered periods is not significantly negative (t-test p > 0.05).
